meta.name: Tibs (Ethiopian Sautéed Meat)
meta.subtitle: Quick-seared beef (or lamb) with onions, peppers, and Ethiopian spices; served sizzling with injera.

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: This file models a flexible tibs baseline with a beef default and lamb variation.
Supports both 'dereq tibs' (drier, high-heat sear) and 'wet tibs' (saucy) approaches via variations.
Spice set uses mitmita/berbere conventions; substitutions provided for availability.

summary: Tibs are Ethiopian sautéed meat—typically quick-seared beef or lamb with onions, peppers, garlic, and berbere/mitmita—often served hot with injera.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a main with injera and sides; 6 as part of a platter.

required.label: Heavy skillet or cast iron pan

optional.label: Instant-read thermometer
optional.notes: Helpful for doneness targeting in SSA.

notes: If using niter kibbeh/ghee, contains dairy.
Serve with injera (typically teff; verify blend if gluten avoidance matters).

ingredients.label: Beef, cut into bite-size cubes (sirloin, ribeye, or chuck steak)
notes: Chuck works well but benefits from a slightly longer cook or a 'wet tibs' variation.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
ingredients.notes: Avocado, grapeseed, canola, or other high-heat oil.

ingredients.label: Niter kibbeh (optional, for Ethiopian richness)
ingredients.notes: Stir in at the end (or replace part of oil).

ingredients.label: Green bell pepper, sliced
ingredients.notes: Common in many home-style tibs; omit for a more meat-forward version.

ingredients.label: Jalapeño or Ethiopian green chile, sliced
ingredients.notes: Adjust heat to preference; remove seeds for milder.

ingredients.label: Berbere spice blend
notes: Typical range: 8–16 g depending on blend heat and desired color.
Add after searing so it doesn’t scorch.

ingredients.label: Mitmita (optional, for extra heat)
ingredients.notes: Start small; mitmita can be very hot.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Split between seasoning the meat and finishing to taste.

notes: Key to great tibs: very hot pan + dry meat + cook in batches to avoid steaming.
If you want the classic sizzling presentation, heat the pan until very hot and serve immediately.
Berbere can burn easily—add once the pan is not bone-dry, and stir quickly.

changes.protein: Replace beef with lamb shoulder or leg cubes (same weight).
changes.notes: Lamb pairs especially well with berbere; consider adding a little extra niter kibbeh at the end.

changes.technique: Cook meat in smaller batches; reduce vegetables; keep sauce minimal.
changes.notes: Aim for browned edges and minimal moisture.

changes.add: Add 120 ml water or stock after spices; simmer 6–10 minutes until slightly saucy.
changes.notes: Useful when using tougher cuts like chuck.

changes.veg: Omit bell pepper and jalapeño; add 50 g more onion instead.

notes: For beef cubes: cook to preference; for food safety, sear thoroughly and ensure surfaces are browned.
If using lamb: same approach; ensure thorough sear.
Best fresh; leftovers reheat well in a skillet with a splash of water.
Not ideal for freezing due to texture changes.

fridge.notes: Reheat in a skillet on medium with a small splash of water; avoid microwaving too long (toughens meat).

leftoversPlan: Day 2: Reheat and serve with eggs for a quick breakfast hash.
Day 2: Chop leftovers and fold into a rice bowl with greens.

steps.title: Prep and season the meat
instructions: Cut beef into 2–3 cm bite-size cubes.
Pat dry thoroughly with paper towels (dry meat browns better).
Season with about half the salt and (optional) black pepper.
checks: Meat is cubed, dried, and lightly seasoned.

steps.title: Preheat skillet and sear in batches
instructions: Heat a heavy skillet over medium-high to high heat until very hot.
Sear beef in 2 batches so the pan is not crowded; spread meat in a single layer.
Brown 2–3 minutes per side (or until edges are well seared). Transfer browned meat to a plate.
checks: Meat has browned edges; pan is not flooded with liquid.

steps.title: Sauté onions and peppers
instructions: Add sliced onions (and bell pepper/jalapeño if using).
Sauté 3–5 minutes until onions soften and pick up browned bits from the pan.
checks: Onions softened; fond is lifted into the vegetables.

steps.title: Add aromatics and bloom spices
instructions: Add garlic (and ginger if using). Cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Add berbere (and mitmita if using). Stir constantly for 20–40 seconds to bloom without burning.
checks: Spices smell fragrant; none are scorched.

steps.title: Return meat and finish cooking
instructions: Return the browned beef (and any juices) to the skillet. Toss to coat.
Cook 2–5 minutes until beef reaches your preferred doneness and everything is well coated.
checks: Meat is hot, coated, and cooked to preference.

steps.title: Optional finishes and serve
instructions: Optional: stir in niter kibbeh off heat for extra aroma and richness.
Optional: squeeze a little lemon juice to brighten.
Garnish with cilantro if desired and serve immediately with injera.
checks: Tibs are glossy, aromatic, and ready to serve.

change.notes: Use 6–8 g berbere, omit mitmita, and add peppers only if desired.
Skip niter kibbeh/ghee; finish with lemon instead.
Use tender beef (sirloin/ribeye), skip peppers, and reduce onion quantity to 150 g.
